_README: BLANK TEMPLATE — the contract every AI fills for one country. Do NOT change field NAMES or STRUCTURE; fill VALUES only. Keep every key (use null if you can't fill it). TWO data types, reconciled differently: (1) FACTS — objective checkable values. (2) RECOMMENDATIONS — judgments with no single right answer. STATUS MODEL (set 'status' honestly per fact): 'verified' = you confirmed it against an OFFICIAL source (put the URL in source_url); 'agreed_stable' = a stable fact you're confident on but didn't open a source for (capital, currency code, emergency numbers); 'verify_recommended' = change-prone or high-stakes (visa rules/fees, advisories, entry rules) — flag it; 'serve_live' = volatile, must NOT be stored as a fact (exchange rate, flight prices). For RECOMMENDATIONS use rank 'core' (your strong picks) — no status needed. Return ONLY valid JSON in EXACTLY this structure. Fill EVERY section — do not skip any.
_fact_field_shape: Every FACT uses: { value, source_url, status (verified|agreed_stable|verify_recommended|serve_live), notes }. For 'verified' the source_url MUST be the official page you checked. Never invent a source.

visa._note: YMYL + change-prone. Try to set 'verified' against the official consular/eVisa site; otherwise 'verify_recommended'.

type.notes: US citizens eligible for visa-free entry for short-term tourism/business up to 90 days

fee.notes: No visa fee for visa-waiver entry

processing_time.notes: No processing required — visa waiver granted on arrival

online_authorization_required.notes: Japan plans a digital travel authorization system (similar to ESTA) — not yet implemented as of 2026

value: Any site charging a fee for 'Japan visa waiver registration' — official visa waiver has no fee

passport_validity_required.notes: Japan requires passport valid for intended stay period; airlines may require 3-6 months beyond stay

onward_ticket_required.notes: Proof of onward/return ticket may be requested by immigration

notes.value: US citizens should carry passport with blank pages for entry stamp

cash_vs_card.value: Cash still widely used, especially outside major cities and for small purchases. Credit cards accepted at hotels, department stores, and larger restaurants. IC cards (Suica/Pasmo) for transit and convenience stores.

atm_availability.value: ATMs at 7-Eleven, Japan Post Bank, and Lawson accept international cards. Bank ATMs often do not.

currency_import_export_limit.value: Over 1 million JPY (or equivalent) must be declared

tipping_norm.value: Not practiced — can be considered rude. Exceptional service is standard; no tip expected.

main_international_airports.notes: US travelers primarily use NRT and HND for Tokyo

flight_from_audience_origins.notes: Flight durations are stable estimates; prices are VOLATILE — serve live

intercity_rail.value: Shinkansen (bullet train) — efficient, punctual, covers major cities. Tokyo–Kyoto ~2h15m (~14,000 JPY). Japan Rail Pass available for tourists (must purchase before arrival).

buses.value: Highway buses cheaper than trains (Tokyo–Kyoto ~5,000-8,000 JPY). Local buses within cities use IC cards or cash.

domestic_flights.value: Available but less practical than Shinkansen for Tokyo–Kyoto–Osaka corridor

ride_sharing.value: Limited; Uber exists but mostly connects to licensed taxis. GO and DiDi taxi apps available.

car_rental_and_driving.value: Left-side driving. International Driving Permit (IDP) required for US license holders. Not recommended for Tokyo/Kyoto (excellent public transit). Useful for rural areas like Hokkaido or Shikoku.

climate_by_season.value: Spring (Mar–May): mild, cherry blossoms. Summer (Jun–Aug): hot, humid, rainy (June). Fall (Sep–Nov): mild, autumn colors. Winter (Dec–Feb): cold, dry; snow in north/central mountains.

best_months.notes: Peak travel seasons — book far in advance

avoid_periods.notes: Domestic travel peaks — accommodations and transport extremely crowded and expensive

safety_health._note: YMYL. Advisory level is change-prone → verify_recommended. Stable items (emergency numbers) → agreed_stable.

advisory_level.notes: US State Dept advisory as of May 2026. Verify current level before travel.

main_risk.value: Natural disasters (earthquakes, typhoons June–Oct). Low violent crime; petty theft rare but possible in tourist areas.

vaccines.value: No mandatory vaccines for US citizens. Routine vaccines (MMR, Tdap, hepatitis A recommended by CDC).

value: Roppongi (Tokyo) — inflated bar bills and touts
Overpriced 'free' temple tours that demand donations
Fake monks soliciting money at tourist sites

sim_esim.value: eSIM widely available (povo, Airalo, Ubigi). Pocket WiFi rentals at airports. Physical SIMs at airports and electronics stores.
sim_esim.notes: povo Japan SIM example: 3GB/3d = 600 JPY, 20GB/30d = 2,700 JPY

coverage.value: Excellent 4G/5G coverage in cities and along major transit routes. Rural areas may have gaps.

dress_norms.value: Conservative: covering shoulders/knees for temples and shrines. Casual is fine in cities. Onsen (hot spring) requires full nudity in gender-separated baths.

value: DO bow when greeting (casual nod is fine)
DO remove shoes before entering homes, ryokans, some restaurants, and temple buildings
DO say 'Itadakimasu' before eating and 'Gochisousama' after
DO NOT eat or drink while walking
DO NOT stick chopsticks upright in rice (funeral ritual)
DO NOT talk on phones on trains (mute phones, no calls)
DO NOT enter a private onsen with tattoos (many ban them; check first)

how_to_get_there.value: Fly into Narita (NRT) or Haneda (HND). Narita: 60-90 min by Narita Express (N'EX, ~3,000 JPY) or Skyliner (~2,500 JPY). Haneda: 30-40 min by Keikyu Line or Tokyo Monorail (~500-600 JPY).

local_transport.value: Extensive subway and train network (Tokyo Metro, Toei, JR East). Suica/Pasmo IC cards essential (tap in/out). 24-48 hr metro passes available for tourists. Avoid 7:30-9:30 AM and 5:30-7:30 PM rush hours.

airport.value: Haneda (HND) — closer to city; Narita (NRT) — farther but major int'l hub

value.name: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
value.why: Best garden in Tokyo — peaceful contrast to neon madness

value.name: Senso-ji (Asakusa)
value.why: Tokyo's oldest temple with vibrant Nakamise-dori market street

value.name: Shibuya Crossing & Hachiko
value.why: Iconic — watch the scramble from Starbucks or MAGNET roof

value.name: TeamLab Planets (Toyosu)
value.why: Immersive digital art museum — book weeks in advance

value.name: Meiji Jingu (Shibuya)
value.why: Serene forest shrine dedicated to Emperor Meiji

value.name: Tsukiji Outer Market
value.why: Fresh seafood breakfast, grilled tuna, tamagoyaki, and knife shops

top_sights.notes: Priority 2-3 days: Asakusa + Sumida River / West Tokyo (Shinjuku/Shibuya) / Imperial Palace + Ginza

value.who_for: Nightlife, shopping, energy — first-timers love the neon chaos
Youth culture, fashion, crossing — great for 20s-30s travelers
Traditional atmosphere, quieter, Senso-ji nearby — budget to mid-range
Luxury shopping, fine dining, upscale — higher budgets
Museums, park, budget hotels, easy access to Narita via Skyliner

where_to_stay.notes: Book 3-4 months ahead for cherry blossom/autumn

value.why: Small shared dishes, great vibe — chains like Torikizoku or local spots
Ichiran (individual booths) or local shops like Afuri (yuzu shio)
Mantenzushi (lunch deals) or conveyor belt (Sushiro/Kura)
Maisen (Harajuku/Aoyama) or Tonki (Meguro)
Mind-blowing bento boxes and sweets — eat in park

where_to_eat.notes: Reservations for high-end; convenience stores (Family Mart, 7-Eleven) surprisingly good for cheap eats

value.why: Giant Buddha (Daibutsu), bamboo forest, beaches. 1 hour from Tokyo.
Toshogu Shrine (elaborate carvings, 'see no evil' monkeys). 2 hours.
Onsen, views of Mt Fuji (on clear days), pirate ship on Lake Ashi. Part of Fuji-Hakone-Izu National Park.
Cup Noodles Museum, Chinatown, Minato Mirai waterfront. 30 min.

day_trips.notes: Kamakura and Hakone are top picks for first-timers

how_to_get_there.value: Shinkansen from Tokyo: Nozomi (2h15m, ~14,000 JPY) or Hikari (2h40m, covered by JR Pass if prepurchased). From Tokyo Station to Kyoto Station.

local_transport.value: Buses are primary for temples/shrines (¥230 flat fare). Subway limited. Get 1-2 day bus pass (¥700-1,200). IC cards (Suica/Icoca) work on buses. Renting bikes is excellent. Taxis are plentiful but expensive.

airport.value: No airport — nearest is Itami (ITM, domestic) or Kansai International (KIX, int'l — 1.5 hours by train/bus)

value.name: Fushimi Inari Shrine
value.why: Thousands of red torii gates snaking up Mt Inari — go at 7 AM to avoid crowds

value.name: Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion)
value.why: Zen temple covered in gold leaf, mirrored in pond — classic Japan photo

value.name: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
value.why: Surreal bamboo forest — arrive before 8 AM or at sunset

value.name: Kiyomizu-dera
value.why: Wooden stage overlooking Kyoto — best view of city and cherry blossoms

value.name: Gion District
value.why: Traditional wooden machiya houses — chance spotting of geiko/maiko

value.name: Philosopher's Path
value.why: Canal path lined with cherry trees — best during sakura or autumn

top_sights.notes: 2-3 days ideal: Southern Higashiyama (Kiyomizu) day 1, Northern temples (Kinkaku-ji) + Arashiyama day 2

value.who_for: Best dining, shopping, nightlife — central for all sights
Traditional atmosphere, walking distance to temples — atmospheric but pricier
Convenience for Shinkansen/buses — less charming but practical
Relaxed, nature-focused — great for ryokan stay away from city center

where_to_stay.notes: Stay near Kawaramachi or Kyoto Station for easiest transit

value.why: Kyoto specialty — splurge lunch costs less than dinner (3,000-8,000 JPY vs 15,000+)
Zen Buddhist cuisine — Okutan near Nanzen-ji is historic
Uji (south of Kyoto) is matcha capital — Tsujiri or Nakamura Tokichi
Food market — try yuba (tofu skin), pickles, tamagoyaki skewers
Small Kyoto-style side dishes — find in Pontocho alley

where_to_eat.notes: Nishiki Market = food stroll (don't eat while walking)

value.why: Todai-ji Temple (world's largest wooden building + giant Buddha) + 1,000+ sacred deer in Nara Park. 45 min from Kyoto.
Matcha capital, Byodoin Temple (on 10-yen coin), scenic river. 20 min.
Dotonbori street food, Osaka Castle, neon chaos. 30 min.
value.rough_time: Full day or evening for food

value.why: Japan's most beautiful original castle (White Heron). 30-60 min by Shinkansen.

day_trips.notes: Nara is unmissable; best combined with morning in Kyoto

itineraries.title: Japan in 7 Days: First-Timer's Tokyo + Kyoto

value.morning: Arrive at Narita/Haneda. Pick up Suica, pocket WiFi/eSIM, exchange currency.
value.afternoon: Check in Shinjuku or Shibuya hotel. Explore Shinjuku — Omoide Yokocho (Memory Lane), Tokyo Metro Govt Building (free night view).
value.evening: Dinner in Shinjuku izakaya. Jet lag early bed.

value.morning: Shibuya Crossing and Hachiko. Meiji Jingu shrine (peaceful forest).
value.evening: Shibuya Sky or MAGNET roof for Shibuya scramble night view. Dinner in Shibuya.

value.morning: Asakusa — Senso-ji temple + Nakamise-dori. Free sake tasting at nearby breweries.
value.afternoon: Sumida River cruise to Hamarikyu Garden (tea house). Option: TeamLab Planets (book ahead).
value.evening: Ginza dinner (or Tokyo Station Ramen Street for budget).

value.morning: Morning: Shinkansen to Kyoto (2h15m, reserve seats day before). Drop bags near Kyoto Station.
value.afternoon: Fushimi Inari (torii gates — climb at least halfway).
value.evening: Check in near Kawaramachi. Pontocho alley dinner.

value.morning: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove (arrive by 7:30 AM). Tenryu-ji temple + Togetsu-kyo Bridge.
value.afternoon: Monkey Park Iwatayama (view of Kyoto). Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion).
value.evening: Dinner in Gion; walk Hanami-koji to spot geiko.

value.afternoon: Philosopher's Path + Ginkaku-ji (Silver Pavilion). Or Nara day trip (30 min by train).
value.evening: Last Kyoto dinner — kaiseki or yudofu.

value.morning: Nishiki Market breakfast and food souvenirs.
value.afternoon: Shinkansen back to Tokyo for departure flight (or depart from KIX).

plan.notes: Assumes US traveler with jet lag tolerance. Can swap day 2/3 order. Purchase JR Pass before arrival if doing additional Shinkansen trips (not needed for just Tokyo–Kyoto round trip — cost about same as individual tickets).

assumptions.value: US passport (visa-free). Moderate fitness (lots of walking). Mid-range budget ($150-250/day excluding flights). Spring (Mar-Apr) or fall (Oct-Nov) timing.

value.name: Kyoto's Kinkaku-ji
value.why: Iconic gold-leaf pavilion — the single most photographed temple in Japan.

value.name: Tokyo's Shibuya Crossing
value.why: No place on earth like it — pure organized chaos.

value.name: Miyajima (Itsukushima Shrine)
value.why: Torii gate in the water at high tide — magical. Extend if time.

value.name: Japanese Alps (Kamikochi/Takayama)
value.why: World-class mountain scenery, onsen, traditional villages. 3-4 day add-on.

value.name: Osaka street food
value.why: Dotonbori — takoyaki (octopus balls) and okonomiyaki (savory pancake).

must_see_overall.notes: Prioritize Kyoto + Tokyo for first trip under 10 days. Add Hiroshima/Miyajima with 10-14 days.

_open_questions._note: Anything genuinely unsure / conflicting / that needs a real source. Be honest — this is more useful than false confidence.
value: Japan's planned digital travel authorization system (similar to ESTA) has an uncertain implementation date — current sources indicate it's coming but not yet required for US citizens as of May 2026. Check closer to travel.

US State Department advisory level for Japan: credible news reports indicate Level 1 as of May 2026, but official State Dept website not directly cited in search results. Verify directly at travel.state.gov before travel.
JR Pass price increases effective October 2023 (substantial hike). Search results had conflicting information; verify current pricing if recommending JR Pass.
Tattoo policies in onsen (hot springs) vary widely — some ban entirely, others allow with covering, some tattoo-friendly. Need specific onsen recommendations for travelers with tattoos.
Golden Week 2027 dates: April 29–May 5 (needs yearly verification).
